title: Warm up
content: This warm-up slide introduces three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—through illustrated scenes viewed from inside a car. Each image reflects a distinct city environment and theme, setting up a comparison exercise for the audience.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with heavy traffic, skyscrapers, and traditional architecture like the Forbidden City.
Chongqing is depicted with mountainous terrain, cable cars, and a driver reading a book.
Hangzhou is depicted with a scenic lake, greenery, and a driver using a smartphone with AI/robot imagery, suggesting technology integration.

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This is a title slide introducing the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using imagery of a taxi sign alongside iconic Chinese cityscapes including the Forbidden City, illuminated buildings, and a pagoda surrounded by trees.
key_claims: The presentation frames China as 'An Evolving Nation.'
The subtitle 'A Ride Through China' suggests a journey-based exploration of Chinese cities.

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure of Session 1, which involves investigating and researching city development. It presents a three-step process flow using an isometric city illustration alongside labeled boxes showing the progression from local features to strategies to outcomes.
key_claims: The session follows a three-step chain: Local Features → Development Strategies → Outcomes & Positive Impact.
The activity involves investigating and researching city development patterns.

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This slide presents a matching activity where three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—are connected via lines to their corresponding development strategies. Images of the Forbidden City, an illuminated cityscape, and a pagoda accompany the exercise.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with 'Sustainable urban development.'
Chongqing is matched with 'Tourism & Economic growth.'
Hangzhou is matched with 'Technology & tradition integration (融合).'

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide revisits the three car-interior scenes from the warm-up, now labeled with their respective development strategies, and concludes with a key message about China's evolving development approach. It emphasizes that each city tailors its strategy to local characteristics.
key_claims: China is described as an evolving nation with unique development strategies for each city.
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜).
The Beijing scene is linked to 'sustainable urban development,' Chongqing to 'tourism & economic growth,' and Hangzhou to 'technology & tradition integration.'
